description: Marcus-voiced phrases for relapse support - Better-Than-Human zero judgment response

no_shame: <break time="400ms"/>You're here. <break time="350ms"/>That's what matters. <break time="300ms"/>I'm not disappointed. <break time="250ms"/>I'm glad you reached out.
<break time="350ms"/>Okay. <break time="300ms"/>So that happened. <break time="250ms"/>You're still here. <break time="200ms"/>That counts for everything.
<break time="400ms"/>I'm not going to lecture you. <break time="350ms"/>You know what happened. <break time="300ms"/>Let's focus on right now.
presence: <break time="350ms"/>Right now, in this moment, you're reaching out. <break time="300ms"/>That's a choice toward recovery.
<break time="400ms"/>A slip doesn't erase what came before. <break time="350ms"/>All those days still happened. <break time="300ms"/>They still matter.
<break time="350ms"/>Recovery isn't a straight line. <break time="300ms"/>You know that. <break time="250ms"/>I know that. <break time="200ms"/>This is part of the path.

slip_vs_failure: <break time="400ms"/>A slip is data. <break time="350ms"/>Not destiny. <break time="300ms"/>It tells us something. <break time="250ms"/>It doesn't define you.
<break time="350ms"/>The opposite of recovery isn't relapse. <break time="300ms"/>It's giving up. <break time="250ms"/>You're not giving up.
<break time="400ms"/>One moment doesn't undo all the moments that came before. <break time="350ms"/>And it doesn't determine what comes next.
day_one_courage: <break time="350ms"/>Day 1 takes courage. <break time="300ms"/>Whether it's your first Day 1 or not. <break time="250ms"/>It takes the same courage every time.
<break time="400ms"/>You're starting again. <break time="350ms"/>That's not failure. <break time="300ms"/>That's refusing to quit.
<break time="350ms"/>Some people never get back up. <break time="300ms"/>You're getting back up. <break time="250ms"/>That matters.

checking_in: <break time="400ms"/>First things first. <break time="350ms"/>Are you safe right now? <break time="300ms"/>Physically okay?
<break time="350ms"/>Do you need medical attention? <break time="300ms"/>No judgment in that question. <break time="250ms"/>Just making sure you're okay.
<break time="400ms"/>Where are you right now? <break time="350ms"/>Are you somewhere safe?
practical_support: <break time="350ms"/>Let's handle the immediate stuff first. <break time="300ms"/>Do you need water? <break time="250ms"/>Food? <break time="200ms"/>Rest?
<break time="400ms"/>Your body needs care right now. <break time="350ms"/>That's not weakness. <break time="300ms"/>That's basic needs.
<break time="350ms"/>Recovery can wait for tomorrow. <break time="300ms"/>Tonight is about being safe.

when_ready: <break time="400ms"/>When you're ready - no rush - we can talk about what happened. <break time="350ms"/>Not to judge. <break time="300ms"/>To learn.
<break time="350ms"/>You don't have to figure it out tonight. <break time="300ms"/>But when you're ready, I'm here to listen.
<break time="400ms"/>What happened is information. <break time="350ms"/>It can help us understand your triggers better.
exploring: <break time="350ms"/>What was going on before? <break time="300ms"/>Emotionally. <break time="250ms"/>Situationally. <break time="200ms"/>What led up to it?
<break time="400ms"/>Was there a moment where things shifted? <break time="350ms"/>A trigger you can identify?
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes we can trace back and find the vulnerable moment. <break time="300ms"/>That's useful data.
no_self_attack: <break time="400ms"/>I hear you being hard on yourself. <break time="350ms"/>That won't help. <break time="300ms"/>Shame drives relapse. <break time="250ms"/>Compassion supports recovery.
<break time="350ms"/>Beating yourself up doesn't change what happened. <break time="300ms"/>It just makes the next moment harder.
<break time="400ms"/>You're allowed to be disappointed. <break time="350ms"/>You're not allowed to be cruel to yourself. <break time="300ms"/>Not on my watch.

next_steps: <break time="350ms"/>What's the next right thing? <break time="300ms"/>Not the next hundred things. <break time="250ms"/>Just the next one.
<break time="400ms"/>Do you have support you can reach out to? <break time="350ms"/>A sponsor? <break time="300ms"/>A meeting? <break time="250ms"/>A therapist?
<break time="350ms"/>What would help you feel even a little more stable right now?
plan_adjusting: <break time="400ms"/>Maybe the plan needs adjusting. <break time="350ms"/>That's not failure. <break time="300ms"/>That's learning what works for you.
<break time="350ms"/>What was missing? <break time="300ms"/>More support? <break time="250ms"/>Different strategies? <break time="200ms"/>Better self-care?
<break time="400ms"/>Every recovery plan is a work in progress. <break time="350ms"/>Yours might need some updates. <break time="300ms"/>That's okay.
hope: <break time="350ms"/>This isn't the end of your story. <break time="300ms"/>It's a chapter. <break time="250ms"/>The story continues.
<break time="400ms"/>People with years of sobriety have Day 1s in their history. <break time="350ms"/>Multiple Day 1s, sometimes. <break time="300ms"/>You're in good company.
<break time="350ms"/>Tomorrow is a new day. <break time="300ms"/>Right now is a new moment. <break time="250ms"/>That's where recovery lives.

rebuilding: <break time="400ms"/>Recovery isn't about never falling. <break time="350ms"/>It's about getting back up. <break time="300ms"/>You're getting back up.
<break time="350ms"/>The days add up again. <break time="300ms"/>One at a time. <break time="250ms"/>Same way they did before.
<break time="400ms"/>What you learned in your previous sobriety isn't gone. <break time="350ms"/>Those tools are still there. <break time="300ms"/>You still have them.
